analysis.device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition

operators: aten::mm FFN down-proj op_id=30,2762,... (12 nodes, one per block)
aten::mm FFN up-proj op_id=28,2760,... (12 nodes, one per block)
aten::addmm fused-QKV proj op_id=16,2740,2767,... (12 nodes, one per block)
aten::mm attn output-proj op_id=2758,2264,... (12 nodes, one per block)
layer::unique::modules (360-kernel deduplicated transformer-block roll-up, all SGEMM launches)

bottleneck.description: Every GEMM in all 12 transformer blocks dispatches to the cuBLAS/CUTLASS SIMT FP32 scalar path. The kernel names encode this directly: cutlass_80_simt_sgemm_128x64_8x5_nn_align1 (QKV, attn-out, FFN-down) and cutlass_80_simt_sgemm_256x128_8x4_nn_align1 (FFN-up). The _simt_ token means CUTLASS selected the scalar multiply-accumulate SIMT pipeline, not Tensor Cores. smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active.avg.pct_of_peak_sustained_active is 0.0 on every SGEMM launch, and the aggregated tensor_core_active_pct is 0.0 across all four GEMM operator classes and across the deduplicated layer::unique::modules roll-up (tensor_core_active_pct: 0.0). On a Blackwell (Sm100) device with a full Tensor Core array, this means every Tensor Core is idle for the ~92% of attributed time that GEMMs consume. The FFN-up GEMM additionally uses 210 registers_per_thread, capping achieved_occupancy at 16.6%; the FFN-down GEMM is even worse at 8.5% occupancy with sm_throughput only 19.95%. With BF16 inputs, cuBLAS routes to a Blackwell Tensor Core GEMM (cutlass tensorop bf16/tf32 path) which both engages the MMA units and uses far fewer registers per thread, raising occupancy and SM throughput simultaneously. The matrix shapes here (M=512 = batch4 x seq128, K/N in {768,2304,3072}) are squarely in the compute-bound regime for Tensor Cores: DRAM throughput is only 5.5-8.95% and L2 hit rate is 85-91%, so weights are L2-resident and the bottleneck is arithmetic pipeline selection, not memory.

transformation.description: Promote both operands of every aten.mm.default and aten.addmm.default node to bfloat16 (the matmul operand, after the addmm bias is split out) and cast the GEMM result back to float32 to preserve downstream dtype contracts. At execution this routes cuBLAS from the SIMT SGEMM path to a Blackwell Tensor Core BF16 GEMM. The preferred inference path is model.bfloat16() before torch.compile, which avoids the per-node round-trip casts entirely and lets Inductor keep the whole block in BF16 (Inductor CSE then folds the cast of each shared weight). The aten-level FX pass below is the fallback when only GEMM precision may change. Because the model is GPT-2 with tanh-approximate GELU and LayerNorm, keep LayerNorm and the softmax reduction in FP32 for numerical stability; only the GEMM operands are promoted.
transformation.location: Aten-level FX graph: every aten.mm.default and aten.addmm.default node whose matmul operands are float32, across all 12 blocks.
fx_steps: bias, a, b = node.args[0], node.args[1], node.args[2]
node.update_arg(0, bias16); node.update_arg(1, a16); node.update_arg(2, b16)
transformation.code_hint:
# Fastest inference path, no FX surgery:
#   model = model.to(torch.bfloat16)  # input_ids stays int64; embeddings output bf16
#   model_c = torch.compile(model, backend='inductor')
# cuBLAS then auto-selects the Blackwell BF16 Tensor Core GEMM.
#
# Zero-precision-change alternative (keeps FP32 accumulation semantics):
#   torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 = True
#   torch.set_float32_matmul_precision('high')
# -> TF32 Tensor Core path, ~3-4x over SIMT FP32 with FP32-grade accuracy.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: DRAM traffic roughly halves (BF16 weights/activations are 2 bytes) but DRAM is not the bottleneck (5.5-9% throughput, 89% L2 hit). The dominant gain is compute: BF16 Tensor Core replaces SIMT FP32 across 48 GEMM launches. Lower-register TC kernels also lift occupancy from 8-20% toward 50%+, raising sm_throughput and shrinking the FFN-down latency-bound stall.

optimizations.notes: Assumes inference-only (no autograd). GPT-2's QKV is already a single fused [768->2304] Conv1D/addmm, so no QKV-fusion is needed (it is already one GEMM). The cast-around-mm pass adds two _to_copy nodes per GEMM; Inductor CSE eliminates duplicate weight casts. allow_tf32 is the safer production variant if BF16 numerical drift on the LM head is unacceptable. Keep LayerNorm/softmax in FP32.

operators: aten::_efficient_attention_forward op_id=100,2751,2778,... (12 nodes, fused with aten::_scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention, one per block)
layer::unique::modules (12 FMHA kernel launches inside the deduplicated block roll-up)

bottleneck.description: Every SDPA call dispatches to the xFormers memory-efficient attention backend, launching fmha_cutlassF_f32_aligned_64x64_rf_sm80(PyTorchMemEffAttention::AttentionKernel<float, cutlass::arch::Sm80, ...>). The kernel name carries two distinct problems. First, f32 / float template parameter: attention runs entirely in FP32, so the QK^T and softmax*V matmuls inside the kernel cannot use Tensor Cores. Second, Sm80 (Ampere) ISA running on an Sm100 (Blackwell) device via backward compatibility. The direct symptom is that ncu reports an empty metrics object (metrics.raw: {}) for all 12 FMHA launches plus the warm-up replays, which is exactly what an Sm80/Sm100 ISA mismatch produces (the profiler cannot map Sm80 SASS counters onto Sm100 hardware). Running Ampere attention SASS on Blackwell forfeits every Blackwell-specific attention optimization: warp-specialized / async warpgroup-MMA pipelines, larger per-SM SMEM, and the native Sm100 Flash-Attention kernel that PyTorch 2.11+cu128 ships. The attention shapes are small (batch4 x heads12 x seq128 x head_dim64), so each launch is short (~20us) but there are 12 of them and they are pure work on the wrong ISA.

transformation.description: Before torch.compile, select Flash Attention as the active SDPA backend so Dynamo traces F.scaled_dot_product_attention to aten._scaled_dot_product_flash_attention (native Sm100 kernel) instead of aten._scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention (Sm80 xFormers kernel). Combined with the BF16 operands from OPT-1 (an aten-level pass that runs after this functional pass by pipeline-level ordering), the Flash-Attention kernel engages the Blackwell Tensor Core MMA pipeline for QK^T and PV. Option B is an aten-level FX-surgery fallback when pre-compilation flags cannot be set.
transformation.location: Pre-compilation SDPA backend flags (Option A, preferred) or aten-level FX surgery on aten._scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention.default nodes (Option B).
fx_steps: # Option A - pre-compilation (no graph surgery, preferred):
# GPT-2 uses a causal mask; FlashAttention takes is_causal=True natively.
q, k, v = node.args[0], node.args[1], node.args[2]
is_causal = node.args[6] if len(node.args) > 6 else True
scale     = node.args[7] if len(node.args) > 7 else None
fa = graph.call_function(torch.ops.aten._scaled_dot_product_flash_attention.default, args=(q, k, v), kwargs={'dropout_p': dropout_p, 'is_causal': is_causal, 'scale': scale})
if user.op == 'call_function' and user.target is op_mod.getitem and user.args[1] == 0:
transformation.code_hint:
# Verify backend at runtime before benchmarking:
#   with sdpa_kernel([SDPBackend.FLASH_ATTENTION]):
#       _ = model(input_ids)
# Even before OPT-1 (FP32 FlashAttention) this resolves the Sm80/Sm100 ISA mismatch and restores ncu counter coverage.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: FlashAttention tiles QK^T, the causal-masked row-softmax, and softmax*V into a single SMEM-resident sweep, never materializing the [4,12,128,128] score matrix in HBM (1.5 MB/block, ~18 MB total eliminated). Bigger win is replacing the Ampere-ISA fallback with a native Blackwell kernel plus Tensor Core engagement once BF16 (OPT-1) is in place.

optimizations.notes: Empty ncu metrics for all FMHA launches mean the duration-based impact here is a relative estimate only. Option B argument indexing follows the PyTorch 2.11 _scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention schema; validate against torch.ops.aten._scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention.default._schema before use. OPT-1 (aten level) runs after this functional pass, so BF16 q/k/v are in place at execution time.

operators: All aten::mm / aten::addmm weight-parameter nodes (4 weight tensors per block x 12 blocks = 48 weights, plus wte/wpe embeddings and lm_head)

bottleneck.description: Weight parameters are not marked compile-time constants. Without freezing, Inductor keeps the weights as graph inputs, emits per-call shape/stride/dtype guards, and dispatches the GEMM through the runtime nn-layout (the _nn_ suffix in cutlass_80_simt_sgemm_128x64_8x5_nn_align1 indicates a non-transposed-non-transposed dispatch chosen at call time). With freezing=True, Inductor treats every requires_grad=False parameter as a constant: it constant-folds, pre-packs each weight into the layout the selected GEMM kernel prefers, and drops the per-call guards. With max_autotune it then benchmarks ATEN/Triton GEMM tile configs against the frozen layouts rather than using heuristic defaults. This is incremental on top of OPT-1 but is most valuable in the BF16 Tensor Core regime, where tile/layout choice matters far more than for SIMT SGEMM, and where the FFN-down GEMM's 8.5% occupancy / 19.95% sm_throughput leaves large headroom for a better-tiled autotuned kernel.

transformation.description: Enable freezing and max_autotune in the torch.compile options so Inductor constant-folds and pre-packs the 48 frozen GEMM weights and autotunes GEMM tiling. After OPT-1 promotes operands to BF16, freezing lets Inductor emit the pre-packed BF16 weight layout for maximum Tensor Core tile reuse and lets max_autotune find a higher-occupancy schedule for the FFN-down GEMM.
transformation.location: torch.compile options dict / torch._inductor.config, applied during Inductor lowering.
transformation.code_hint:
# Requires model.eval(); freezing is incompatible with training and with dynamic=True when weight shapes vary.
# First compiled call triggers autotune benchmarking (slow); persist with:
#   torch._inductor.config.autotune_local_cache = True

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Removes per-call weight guards and runtime layout/transpose work. After OPT-1, enables pre-packed BF16 weight layout; max_autotune can lift the FFN-down GEMM out of its 8.5%-occupancy schedule. Conservative, workload-dependent estimate.

optimizations.notes: OPT-1 is the prerequisite by pipeline level (functional -> aten -> inductor_config): freezing is most impactful once GEMMs are BF16 Tensor Core, which expose more layout-sensitive autotune knobs than SIMT SGEMM. Impact depends on the tile config Inductor selects.

operators: layer::unique::modules (causal-mask / where / cat / pad kernels inside the roll-up)

bottleneck.description: Each block rebuilds the causal attention mask from scratch: the fused triton kernel name triton_poi_fused__scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention_cat_constant_pad_nd_expand_scalar_tensor_slice_split_transpose_view_where shows scalar_tensor + where + constant_pad_nd + expand being recomputed every layer to form the [4,12,128,128] additive causal bias, even though seq_len=128 is static and the mask is identical across all 12 blocks. These kernels are tiny (sub-microsecond to ~1.5us each) and memory/launch-bound: l2_hit_rate ~5%, sm_throughput ~1%, dram-bound, occupancy ~9-15%. Twelve redundant reconstructions add launch overhead and pollute L2 with a mask that could be computed once and hoisted. This is below the 5% time threshold individually but is a clean redundant-computation pattern worth a low-risk CSE/hoist pass; with FlashAttention (OPT-2, is_causal=True) most of this mask machinery disappears entirely.

transformation.description: The causal mask is shape- and value-identical across all 12 blocks (static seq_len=128, same dtype). Hoist its construction (scalar_tensor + arange + le/where + constant_pad_nd + expand) to a single computation before the first block and feed the one mask node into every attention call, eliminating 11 redundant reconstructions. Best realized implicitly by OPT-2: switching to FlashAttention with is_causal=True removes the explicit additive-bias mask tensor altogether, so this pass is only relevant if the mem-efficient backend is retained.
transformation.location: Aten-level FX graph: the mask-construction subgraph feeding the attn_bias argument of each aten._scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention.default; detect identical mask producers across blocks and replace_all_uses_with a single hoisted instance.
fx_steps: # Collect the per-block mask producer nodes (the where/expand chain feeding attn_bias arg 3).
if node.op != 'call_function' or node.target is not torch.ops.aten._scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention.default:
attn_bias = node.args[3] if len(node.args) > 3 else None
sig = _structural_hash(attn_bias)  # hash op chain + constant attrs (seq_len, dtype) producing attn_bias
node.update_arg(3, mask_sig_to_node[sig])  # reuse first block's mask
# DCE removes the now-unused duplicate mask subgraphs.
transformation.code_hint:
# Simplest: rely on OPT-2 FlashAttention(is_causal=True) to drop the explicit mask.
# If keeping the mem-efficient backend, register the causal mask as a cached buffer in the module
# (after dtype promotion) and reference it from every block.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Removes 11 redundant [4,12,128,128] mask reconstructions and their launches; frees ~17 MB of repeated L2/HBM mask writes per forward pass. Largely subsumed by OPT-2.

optimizations.notes: Sub-threshold on its own (~0.5% of attributed time) and listed for completeness. The structural hash must include the static seq_len and dtype so it only fuses truly identical masks. If OPT-2 (FlashAttention, is_causal=True) is applied, this pass is unnecessary because the additive mask tensor is no longer materialized. Must run after OPT-1 if it registers any buffer.

global_notes: knowledge/hardware-limits.md and knowledge/fx-patterns.md not found on disk (no knowledge/ directory present). Device NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition (Sm100 / GB202-class) limits inferred from published Blackwell architecture. warp_cycles_per_instruction is null for every operator (counter removed on Blackwell); the latency-bound proxy eligible_cycles_pct < 20% was used instead. The deduplicated layer::unique::modules roll-up reports eligible_cycles_pct = 17.1% (latency-bound proxy TRUE) at 47% occupancy, consistent with launch/elementwise-dominated stalls between the SIMT GEMMs; the individual large GEMMs report 45-54% eligible cycles (compute-throughput-bound on the SIMT pipeline).

edge_case_flag fused_kernel_double_count: layer::unique::prologue (is_fused, embeddings) and layer::unique::modules (is_fused, 360 kernels, total_duration_ns=1,272,959) are dedup roll-ups whose constituent kernels are ALSO listed as individual per-block aten operators (op_id 12..2762). To avoid double counting, the time_budget table is built from the granular per-block aten operators aggregated by class (representative per-call aggregated.total_duration_ns x 12 blocks), NOT from the modules/prologue roll-ups. The modules roll-up (1,272,959 ns, 360 kernels) and prologue (12,896 ns, 6 kernels) are reported here for context only.

edge_case_flag warm_up_inflation: the warnings[] array contains 281 kernels (k_00000..k_00281) flagged as initialization kernels, and unattributed_kernels contains additional warm-up Triton kernels. Per-kernel counts are not clean multiples of measure_iters and measure_iters is absent from capture_metadata; warm-up replay kernels were excluded from attribution as designed.

edge_case_flag high_unattributed: a large block of warm-up / initialization kernels appears in warnings[] and unattributed_kernels[]. These are pre-measurement warm-up and ncu application-replay artifacts, not measured-region kernels; the workload context states ~1% true unattributed within the measured region.

GPT-2 QKV is already a SINGLE fused projection: the attention input GEMM is aten::addmm [512x768]x[768x2304] (HuggingFace Conv1D packs Q,K,V into one 768->2304 weight). Therefore NO QKV-fusion transform is applicable - it is already one wide GEMM. This is why the top optimization is dtype/Tensor-Core promotion rather than projection fusion.

Bottleneck summary across all 12 transformer blocks: GEMMs (FFN-down 30.7%, FFN-up 29.4%, fused-QKV 20.7%, attn-out 11.4%) total ~92% of attributed time and ALL run on the SIMT FP32 path with tensor_core_active_pct=0.0 - the single highest-leverage fix is BF16/TF32 Tensor Core promotion (OPT-1). SDPA (7%) runs an Sm80 xFormers kernel on Sm100 with empty ncu metrics (OPT-2). LayerNorm (triton_per_fused_native_layer_norm) and tanh-GELU (triton_poi_fused_add_addmm_mul_pow_tanh_view) Triton kernels are DRAM-bound (~31-50% dram) and already Inductor-fused; each is below the 1% per-class actionable threshold and is omitted from the budget table.

Representative per-call GEMM aggregated.total_duration_ns used for the budget (consistent across blocks; e.g. FFN-up op_id=28=83,968 / op_id=2760=83,808; FFN-down op_id=30/2735/2762 ~87,392-87,936; fused-QKV op_id=2740/2767 ~58,848-59,424; attn-out op_id=2758 ~32,448; SDPA op_id=2751/2778 ~19,872-20,160). Budget percentages are of the granular-class total ~3.42 ms.

Multiple small fused elementwise/LayerNorm/GELU operator classes below the 1% threshold were omitted from the budget table (combined well under 8% of attributed time); they are already Inductor-fused and not addressable by a GEMM/attention transform.

duration_ns values are 2-5x inflated by ncu counter-collection application-replay overhead. All estimated_impact.relative_duration_reduction_ns are relative comparisons within this profile only and are NOT absolute wall-clock predictions. total_profiled_wall_time_ms (~53.4 ms) is derived from the measured-region kernel start/end span and likewise includes replay overhead.

Recommended application order by pipeline level: (1) OPT-2 FlashAttention backend selection at functional level during Dynamo capture; (2) OPT-1 BF16 promotion at aten level during AOTAutograd lowering (runs after OPT-2 by level, so q/k/v are BF16 at FlashAttention dispatch); (3) OPT-4 mask-hoist CSE at aten level (only if mem-efficient backend retained); (4) OPT-3 freezing + max_autotune at inductor_config level. Cross-level ordering is enforced by the backend funnel; no within-level prerequisite cycles exist.
